key_insight: Thermal spray metallization enables durable hybrid polymeric–metallic coatings that improve polymer surfaces by combining metallic protection with polymer versatility.
engineering_relevance: These coatings can enhance wear, corrosion, and thermal resistance while also supporting dimensional restoration in polymer-based components.
method: Compares thermal spray routes: cold spray, flame spray, arc spray, and plasma spray
Focuses on process parameters that control adhesion, coating quality, and performance
Highlights cold spray as a particularly effective method for metallizing polymers
evidence_signal: The paper is a review that synthesizes recent literature and evaluates how thermal spray parameters influence hybrid coating properties rather than reporting a single experimental dataset.
watch_item: Strong coating performance depends on careful control of spray temperature, particle velocity, and substrate compatibility to avoid poor adhesion or polymer damage.
paper_title: Thermal Spray Metallization of Polymers: A Review of Hybrid Polymeric–Metallic Coatings
